svg.text: YGB Project Roadmap
Generated from the repository state on 2026-03-22: frozen governance phases, production hardening, Phase 51 platform upgrades, and the documented next-step backlog.
Current Read

The codebase is already beyond a greenfield build. Phases 01-49 are frozen or complete, March audits report production hardening is in place, and Phase 51 adds distributed AI platform capabilities on top.
The roadmap below therefore focuses on stabilization, measurement quality, distributed training retrofit, and multi-node scale-out rather than baseline feature construction.
Completed Foundation
Production Hardening
Next 30 Days
Platform Retrofit
Scale-Out Path
DONE
01. Governance Backbone
Phases 01-49 implemented
• Human authority and immutable phase ordering established
• Frontend, backend, runtime, and evidence model are present
• Execution remains constrained by governance boundaries
• Phase 49 governors formalize controlled runtime behavior
Source anchors: `PHASE_INDEX.md`, `README.md`
Interpretation: the repo already has a mature systems foundation
ACTIVE / VERIFIED
02. Hardened Product Baseline
Audit pass completed in March
• Security remediation items are fixed or mitigated
• Structured logging, readiness probes, and CI gate checks are wired
• 32/32 E2E audit tests passed
• 45/45 remediation validation tests passed
• Remaining observability gap is low-severity ML metric wiring
• Phase 51 extends checkpoints, agent routing, streaming voice, and monitoring
Source anchors: `FULL_AUDIT_REPORT.md`, `VALIDATION_REPORT.md`, `RISK_REGISTER.md`, `PHASE51_PLATFORM_UPGRADE.md`
Next move: convert verified platform pieces into the active training path
NEXT 30 DAYS
03. Model Quality Hardening
Use the existing 4-week plan as the execution sprint
• Week 1: tighten calibration, thresholds, monotonicity, and Brier score
• Week 2: stress drift, thermal guards, and IO recovery behavior
• Week 3: refine duplicate suppression and cross-scope controls
• Week 4: stabilize five-target parallel hunt execution
• Wire `ece`, `drift_kl`, and `duplicate_rate` emitters into the ML pipeline
• Drive toward precision >= 95%, ECE <= 0.02, and zero governance violations
Source anchors: `docs/30_DAY_PLAN.md`, `FULL_AUDIT_REPORT.md` residual risks
This is the clearest near-term roadmap already documented in the repo
ENGINEERING NEXT
04. Platform Retrofit
Move Phase 51 capabilities into the main runtime
• Retrofit `run_real_training.py` to the hardened checkpoint manager
• Add step-aware `global_step` resume coverage
• Introduce distributed engine mapping for DeepSpeed / ZeRO paths
• Add node-failure recovery tests for optimizer, RNG, scaler, and scheduler state
• Keep agent orchestration and streaming voice aligned with governance constraints
Source anchors: `docs/distributed_training_foundation.md`, `PHASE51_PLATFORM_UPGRADE.md`
Interpretation: the upgrade modules exist, but the active path still needs deeper adoption
SCALE OUT
05. Multi-Node Ops
Deferred and scale-specific work
• Persist in-memory workflow state with Redis for multi-node correctness
• Extend checkpoint sync to local NVMe plus background object-store replication
• Strengthen cluster readiness, role assignment, pairing, and mesh validation
• Continue removing broad exception handling in high-risk paths
Source anchors: `RISK_REGISTER.md`, `docs/MODE_A_CHECKLIST.md`, `docs/distributed_training_foundation.md`
These are the steps that turn a hardened single-node system into a resilient cluster
Suggested Execution Order
1. Protect the current wins
Keep the security, observability, and CI baseline green while any training work proceeds.
2. Run the 30-day quality sprint
The repo already defines concrete calibration, drift, duplicate suppression, and parallel-hunt targets. That is the fastest high-confidence roadmap segment.
3. Retrofit distributed foundations into the active path
Checkpoint hardening and DeepSpeed-compatible scaffolding exist; the main training entrypoints still need to consume them consistently.
4. Promote from single-node robustness to cluster resilience
Redis-backed workflow state, object-store replication, and failure-recovery drills are the scale multipliers after model-quality work is stable.
5. Measure what matters
The audit explicitly calls out unfinished emitters for `ece`, `drift_kl`, and `duplicate_rate`; wiring those closes the loop between roadmap goals and runtime evidence.
Completed / frozen
Current verified baseline
Near-term sprint
Platform retrofit
Scale-out backlog
